title: Ensure there are between 2 and 3 subscription owners

input_row.title: Ensure there are between 2 and 3 subscription owners
input_row.description: The Owner role in Azure grants full control over all resources in a subscription, including the ability to assign roles to others.
input_row.rationale: Limit the number of security principals (users, groups, service principals, and managed identities) assigned the Owner role to between 2 and 3. If groups are used, ensure their membership is tightly controlled and regularly reviewed to avoid privilege sprawl.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Subscriptions. 2. Click the name of a subscription. 3. Click Access Controls (IAM). 4. Click Role assignments. 5. Click Role : All. 6. Click the arrow next to All. 7. Click Owner. 8. Ensure a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 3 members are returned. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each subscription. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list members with role Owner at a given subscription scope: az role assignment list --role Owner --scope /subscriptions/ --query "[].{PrincipalName:principalName, Type:principalType}" Ensure a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 3 members are returned. For each Owner assignment, check: If principalType == Group, review group membership. If principalType == ServicePrincipal or ManagedIdentity, validate necessity and scope. Repeat for each subscription. Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list members with role Owner at a given subscription scope: Get-AzRoleAssignment -RoleDefinitionName Owner -Scope /subscriptions/ Ensure a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 3 members are returned. Repeat for each subscription. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 09024ccc-0c5f-475e-9457-b7c0d9ed487b - Name: 'There should be more than one owner assigned to your subscription' • Policy ID: 4f11b553-d42e-4e3a-89be-32ca364cad4c - Name: 'A maximum of 3 owners should be designated for your subscription'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Subscriptions. 2. Click the name of a subscription. 3. Click Access Controls (IAM). 4. Click Role assignments. 5. Click Role : All. 6. Click the arrow next to All. 7. Click Owner. 8. Check the box next to members from whom the owner role should be removed. 9. Click Delete. 10. Click Yes. 11. Repeat steps 1-10 for each subscription requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to delete role assignments by role assignment id: az role assignment delete --ids
input_row.impact: Implementation may require changes in administrative workflows or the redistribution of roles and responsibilities. The recommendation to have between 2 and 3 Owners per subscription must account for all security principals that can be assigned the Owner role, not just individual users. This includes: • User accounts • Entra ID groups • Service principals (used by applications or automation) • Managed identities (system-assigned or user-assigned)
input_row.default_value: A subscription has 1 owner by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit control checks the existence of subscription owners which can be directly queried via API calls. AWS IAM provides a way to manage permission policies, including the checking of roles assigned to a user or group. Specifically, IAM allows for querying role assignments using the `list_roles` and `list_role_policies` methods, making it feasible to validate role ownership programmatically.

Auditing whether there are between 2 and 3 owners assigned to a specific scope can be automated through the AWS API, thus meeting all criteria for an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_iam_user_roles_check_subscription_owners_count

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **API Calls/Boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use `boto3` to call `list_role_assignments()` and check for users assigned to the Owner role at the IAM level.
   - API call to retrieve the role assignments, similar to how Azure checks for owner roles.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - Execute the command: `aws iam list-role-recipients --role-name Owner` to retrieve role assignments at a given scope.

3. **Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Iterate through user accounts or roles to check all IAM users and groups with the Owner role.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Validate the count of users/groups with the Owner role assigned to a particular account or resource.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - If the count of users/groups is less than 2 or greater than 3, then the condition fails.
   - Expected values: Compliant if between 2 and 3 members are assigned to the Owner role.

6. **Example Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - Pass Criteria: 2 <= count of Owner role assignments <= 3
   - Fail Criteria: count < 2 or count > 3

This program will ensure that subscriptions and accounts maintain an optimal number of users with Owner permissions, enhancing security across the organization.
